fresh_facts.claim: President Trump ordered American military assets to remain stationed in the Middle East region around Iran as part of a tentative ceasefire agreement.
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps issued a warning that the Strait of Hormuz may be mined, with notices stating that ships may be hit by anti-ship mines in main transit routes.
Israel conducted major strikes against Lebanon that killed at least 182 people, described as the deadliest day in the latest Israel-Hezbollah conflict.
A two-week conditional ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran was announced with Pakistan mediating negotiations.
President Trump announced the ceasefire deal as contingent on Iran's complete, immediate, and safe opening of the Strait of Hormuz.
Iran presented a 10-point peace proposal that includes withdrawal of U.S. combat forces from regional bases and lifting of all primary and secondary sanctions.
Iran's parliament speaker called the ceasefire unreasonable and accused the U.S. of violating three of Tehran's 10 conditions.
More than 700 individuals had been arrested across various Iranian provinces by late June, with particular focus on Tehran, Kurdish-majority areas, and border regions.
Mahmood Amiry-Moghaddam stated the Islamic Republic is likely to escalate repression to cover up military failures, prevent protests, and ensure its continued survival.
Prince Reza Pahlavi called on Iran's military to step forward against the regime and IRGC, describing the government as driven by destructive and apocalyptic madness.
Increased checkpoints have proliferated in Iranian urban and rural areas with warrantless vehicle searches, phone inspections, and mass detentions reported.
IRGC and Basij militia units expanded operations in Kurdish, Baluchi, and Arab-majority regions of Iran.
Two young men were fatally shot at a checkpoint near Hamedan on July 2.
Vice President JD Vance is leading the U.S. negotiating team for ceasefire talks scheduled to occur in Pakistan.
Iran's deputy foreign minister called Israeli strikes on Lebanon a grave violation.
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu stated that Israel will not stop attacks in Lebanon.
The Israeli military called the coordinated strikes on Lebanon their largest coordinated strike of the current war.
Core disagreements exist regarding whether Lebanon is included in the 14-day truce between the U.S. and Iran.
Core disagreements exist regarding whether the Strait of Hormuz will be reopened as part of the ceasefire.
Evidence indicates Iran has already mined the Strait of Hormuz, as indicated by a released chart showing a danger zone.
Iran's economy has been pushed into an even more unstable and vulnerable state as a result of the conflict.
The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is described as fragile with questions about durability remaining.

regime_fall.current_status: Fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire announced with Pakistan mediation faces immediate strain from continued Israeli strikes on Lebanon and core disagreements over Strait of Hormuz reopening and Lebanon's inclusion, while Iran escalates internal repression and military posturing including Strait mining warnings.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Two-week conditional U.S.-Iran ceasefire announced with Pakistan mediation, contingent on Iran opening the Strait of Hormuz, but core disagreements persist over ceasefire scope and Iranian military has reportedly already mined key transit routes.
